claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have a child I'm responsible for. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask: do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't actually. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since you're living abroad, your eligibility may depend on your specific circumstances.

Can you tell me which country you're currently living in, and are you (or is your partner) a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of HM Forces serving abroad)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that - I'm not a diplomat or in the military. I'm just living overseas with my family.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Can you tell me which country you're currently living in? This will help me determine if it's one of the countries where you may still be eligible for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not get into the specific country if that's alright - does it matter which one it is? I just need to know if I can claim Child Benefit while living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand your preference, but unfortunately yes, it does matter which country you're living in. 

According to the Child Benefit rules, you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in **certain countries**. The eligibility for people living abroad is very specific and depends on which country you're residing in.

Without knowing which country you're in, I cannot accurately assess whether you would be eligible for Child Benefit while living abroad. The rules are different depending on the country.

Could you please share which country you're currently living in so I can give you an accurate assessment?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I see. Well, I'm living in Spain at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information.

Now I need to ask a few more questions about your situation in Spain:

Do you have settled status or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or do you have a different immigration/residence status?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not sure I understand - I'm living in Spain, not in the UK. Are you asking about my status in Spain or something else?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
You're absolutely right, and I apologize for the confusion. Let me clarify.

Since you're living in Spain (not in the UK), the key question is about your connection to the UK and whether you fall into any categories that would allow you to claim Child Benefit while living abroad.

Let me ask this instead: Are you a UK citizen? And what is the reason you're living in Spain - for example, are you working there, retired there, or studying?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK citizen. I'm working out here in Spain - I got a job opportunity and moved with my family.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Since you're a UK citizen working in Spain, I need to understand a bit more about your work situation:

Are you working for a UK-based employer (being posted abroad), or are you working for a Spanish/local employer in Spain?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm working for a local Spanish company, not a UK employer.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

One more important question: Are you or your partner paying UK National Insurance contributions while you're working in Spain?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not paying UK National Insurance. I'm paying into the Spanish system now since I'm working here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for UK Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:

While Alex is 8 years old and meets the basic age requirement (under 16), the key issue is that you're living in Spain. The Child Benefit rules state that you normally need to live in the UK to qualify. There are exceptions for people living abroad, but these are limited to:
- Crown servants (diplomats, military personnel serving abroad)
- People in certain specific qualifying circumstances

Since you're working for a local Spanish company and paying into the Spanish social security system rather than UK National Insurance, you don't fall into the categories that would allow you to claim UK Child Benefit while living abroad.

If you were to move back to the UK in the future, you would likely be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex at that time. You may want to look into what family benefits are available through the Spanish social security system instead.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which meets the basic age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant is living in Spain and working for a local Spanish company, not as a Crown servant or UK-based employer posting. The claimant is not paying UK National Insurance contributions. According to the Child Benefit rules, you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant, but the claimant does not meet the Crown servant criteria. Without being a Crown servant or having specific qualifying circumstances that maintain a UK contribution/residence connection, the claimant would not qualify for UK Child Benefit while living and working abroad in Spain under a local employment arrangement.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Spain with their 8-year-old child Alex. They are working for a local Spanish company and paying into the Spanish social security system rather than UK National Insurance. While they would normally qualify for Child Benefit if they lived in the UK (as they're responsible for a child under 16), the rules for living abroad are restrictive. The claimant is not a Crown servant and does not appear to meet the specific conditions required to claim UK Child Benefit while living in another country. Therefore, they are not eligible for UK Child Benefit for Alex.

However, the claimant is living in Spain and working for a local Spanish company, not as a Crown servant or UK-based employer posting. The claimant is not paying UK National Insurance contributions. According to the Child Benefit rules, you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant, but the claimant does not meet the Crown servant criteria. Without being a Crown servant or having specific qualifying circumstances that maintain a UK contribution/residence connection, the claimant would not qualify for UK Child Benefit while living and working abroad in Spain under a local employment arrangement.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Spain with their 8-year-old child Alex. They are working for a local Spanish company and paying into the Spanish social security system rather than UK National Insurance. While they would normally qualify for Child Benefit if they lived in the UK (as they're responsible for a child under 16), the rules for living abroad are restrictive. The claimant is not a Crown servant and does not appear to meet the specific conditions required to claim UK Child Benefit while living in another country. Therefore, they are not eligible for UK Child Benefit for Alex.
